news.summary: Fluent, Inc. reported unaudited financial results for the first quarter of 2025, showing revenue of $55.2 million, down 16% from Q1 2024. There was a notable growth in Commerce Media Solutions revenue, reaching $12.7 million (a 99% increase from Q1 2024), representing 23% of consolidated revenue. The company's Commerce Media Solutions annual revenue run rate surpassed $65 million, reflecting an 8% quarter-over-quarter increase. Despite a net loss of $8.3 million for Q1 2025, Fluent's CEO expressed confidence in the growth potential of the Commerce Media Solutions business. The company also announced a strategic partnership with Rebuy Engine. Owned and Operated revenue decreased by 30% to $31.1 million. Gross profit margin for Commerce Media Solutions was 22% in Q1 2025. Adjusted EBITDA was negative $3.1 million, 5.6% of revenue. The company aims to establish Commerce Media Solutions as a leader in performance marketing, drive revenue growth, improve net loss, and achieve positive adjusted EBITDA for 2025. Key financials include an increase in Commerce Media Solutions revenue, decrease in Owned and Operated revenue, net loss of $8.3 million, gross profit of $11.4 million, media margin of $13.7 million, and adjusted EBITDA at negative $3.1 million. More details on Fluent, Inc.'s financials can be found in their full report on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Fluent reports Q1 2025 revenue of $55.2 million, 99% growth in Commerce Media Solutions to $12.7 million. Media margin decreased by 38% to $13.7 million. Net loss of $8.3 million. Consolidated revenue decrease of 16% compared to 2024. Company aims to establish CSM business as a leader, improve net loss, and achieve positive adjusted EBITDA for 2025. Conference call to discuss results will be held on May 15, 2025. Safe Harbor Statement underlines forward-looking factors. Total assets stand at $77,494, while total liabilities amount to $55,459.

news.summary: Milestone Scientific Inc. provided a business update and financial results for the first quarter of 2025. The company's revenues for the quarter were approximately $2.2 million, with a slight decrease of $16,000 compared to the previous year. Gross profit was $1.6 million, and the operating loss was approximately $(2.0) million. The net loss was $(2.0) million. As of March 31, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents of approximately $2.2 million and working capital of $2.4 million. Milestone Scientific specializes in developing innovative injection technologies for medical and dental applications. They are focused on expanding their medical segment, particularly the CompuFlo Epidural System. The company is also making efforts to broaden its reimbursement footprint and create long-term value for shareholders. Contact information for further inquiries was provided at the end of the article.
news.headline: Milestone Scientific reports $2.2 million revenue in Q1 2025, with slight decrease amidst progress in medical segment. Loss rose to $(2.0) million; has $2.2 million cash.

news.summary: Nova LifeStyle, Inc. reported their financial results for Q1 2025, with Mr. Xiaohua Lu as the new CEO. Net sales for the quarter were $2.64 million, up 11% from $2.38 million in Q1 2024. This increase was driven by a 26% rise in average selling price, offset slightly by a 12% drop in sales. The higher margin product lines contributed to improved gross profits and margins. The CEO emphasized the company's forward-thinking approach and plans for product line development in 2025. For more details, check the Benzinga.com article.
news.headline: Nova LifeStyle reported Q1 FY 2025 results with $2.64 million in net sales, showing an 11% increase from the previous year. CEO focused on higher margin product lines to improve gross profit.

news.summary: Duos Technologies Group, Inc. reported financial results for Q1 2025, ending March 31, 2025. They recorded over $4.8 million in Services and Consulting revenue, with a significant improvement in Gross Margin compared to the same quarter in 2024. They conducted over 2.3 million railcar scans in Q1 across 13 portals with $17.8 million in revenue backlog and $7.0 - $8.0 million in near-term awards. Total revenues for Q1 2025 increased 363% to $4.95 million compared to $1.07 million in Q1 2024. Cost of revenues increased to $3.64 million, and gross margin increased to $1.31 million. Operating expenses were $3.10 million, leading to a net loss of $2.08 million. Cash and equivalents at March 31, 2025, were $3.80 million. they expect total revenue for 2025 to range between $28 million and $30 million. Duos Technologies Group is confident in its future growth and outlook.
news.headline: Duos Technologies Group's Q1 2025 revenue up 363% to $4.95M ($65K tech systems, $4.89M services). Gross margin up 1,288%.

news.summary: Caliber, a real estate investor, developer, and asset manager, reported results for Q1 2025. Platform revenue was $3.5 million, down from $4.7 million in Q1 2024. Net loss for the platform was $4.1 million, compared to $3.6 million in Q1 2024. Caliber focused on hospitality, multifamily, and multi-tenant industrial real estate. Key milestones included an offering of Series AA Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock, a 1031 Exchange Program launch, and approval of a redevelopment project. A partnership with Hyatt was announced for 15 new hotels. Net loss attributable to Caliber for Q1 2025 was $4.4 million, with Adjusted EBITDA loss of $0.1 million. A conference call discussed financial results. Caliber's managed assets were over $2.9 billion. Forward-looking statements were issued, and GAAP consolidated financial statements were impacted by deconsolidation of certain entities. Non-GAAP reconciliations were provided, and contact for Investor Relations was shared. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Caliber reported Q1 2025 results, with $3.5 million in platform revenue compared to $4.7 million in Q1 2024. Platform net loss was $4.1 million, compared to $3.6 million in Q1 2024. They announced various partnerships and developments. Total revenue was $7.3 million vs. $23.0 million (Q1 2024). Earnings call scheduled.

news.summary: Airship AI Holdings, Inc. reported strong financial results for the first quarter of 2025, with net revenues of $5.5 million, gross profit of $2.2 million, and gross margin of 40%. The company incurred an operating loss of $1.7 million due to increased investments in sales and marketing. Other income was $25.4 million, leading to a net income of $23.7 million. Cash used in operating activities was $2.1 million, with cash and cash equivalents standing at $8.8 million. The backlog was $2.0 million, and the validated pipeline was approximately $135 million. The company aims for 30% revenue growth in 2025 and positive cash flow. Management is confident in achieving these goals through strategic investments, product expansions, and market outreach. Airship AI focuses on digital transformation, AI-driven solutions, and expanding into new markets while closely monitoring tariff developments and maintaining strong supply chain management. The company's balance sheet as of March 31, 2025, showed total assets of $12.9 million and liabilities of $2.2 million. Overall, Airship AI aims for sustained growth through innovation and market expansion strategies.
news.headline: Airship AI's Q1 2025 results show $5.5M net revenues, $2.2M gross profit, 40% margin, $23.7M net income, and $8.8M cash. Expansion plans for the year include targeting 30% revenue growth, new product offerings and partnerships.

news.summary: BIO-key International, Inc. reported financial results for Q1'25, with a 10% sequential revenue increase, reaching $1,607,159, despite a 25% YoY decrease. Gross margin stood at 83%, while operating expenses were reduced by 23% YoY. Net loss in Q1'25 increased to $736,545. The balance sheet improved with total current assets of $4.6M, including $3.1M in cash. The company announced key highlights, partnerships, and deployments during the quarter, aiming for growth in EMEA markets and focusing on government and educational sectors. These strategic moves aim to enhance profitability and cash flow in 2025. The investor call will provide further insights. BIO-key continues to focus on its biometric solutions to meet security challenges across various sectors, highlighting an increasing interest in its IAM solutions. The company remains optimistic about its growth outlook, emphasizing the need to adapt to customer demands and market trends.
news.headline: BIO-key reported Q1'25 revenue of $1.6M with a cash position of $3.1M. Focus on high-margin products in EMEA. Loss increased to $736,545, gross margin at 83%. Key highlights include new partnerships and deployments. Balance sheet shows improvement, total current assets at $4.6M. Strong outlook for growth in EMEA and government markets. Conference call details announced for May 16th at 10am ET. BIO-key's unique biometric solutions are gaining traction in defense/security and financial services sectors. Transition to in-house products affecting revenue but expected growth in 2026. Multiple revenue streams and cost reduction efforts to support path to profitability.

news.summary: KULR Technology Group, Inc., a global energy management solutions leader, reported Q1 results with revenue up 40% at $2.45 million, driven by a product sales increase of 88.7%. Cash and accounts receivable stood at $27.59 million. Gross margin was 8% compared to 29% last year, due to increased labor hours. Operating loss was $9.44 million, net loss was $18.81 million, driven by bitcoin holding mark-to-market. R&D expenses rose to $2.45 million. The company did not renew a San Diego lease, consolidating in Texas. KULR won a $6.7 million grant for space battery tech from the Texas Space Commission. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: KULR Technology Group reported Q1 2025 financial results: Revenue increased by 40% to $2.45 million, with over $100M in cash holding, gross margins at 8%, operating loss at $9.44 million, and net loss at $18.81 million. The Company has consolidated operations into Webster, Texas, and was awarded $6.7M by the Texas Space Commission for advancing cold-temperature space battery technology in collaboration with NASA. Total SG&A expenses were $7.20 million, and R&D expense was $2.45 million in Q1 2025.

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, Firsthand Technology Value Fund, Inc. revealed its financial results for the quarter ending on March 31, 2025. The Fund, which focuses on investing in technology and cleantech companies, reported net assets of about $0.8 million, translating to $0.12 per share by the end of March 2025. This represents a decline from the previous quarter's net assets of roughly $1.1 million, or $0.15 per share by the close of December 2024. The Fund's portfolio by March 31, 2025, comprised public and private securities valued at around $0.7 million, or $0.10 per share, which notably includes approximately $0.07 per share in cash and cash equivalents. Specifically, the investment fair value for equity/debt investments was $0.24 million, equivalent to $0.04 per share, with cash/cash equivalents at $0.45 million, reflecting $0.07 per share, and other assets at $0.79 million, or $0.12 per share.
news.headline: Firsthand Technology Value Fund Q1 2025 results: NAV of $0.12 per share, net assets at $0.8 million, portfolio at $0.7 million.

news.summary: Innventure, Inc. announced financial results for the quarter ending March 31, 2025, with a focus on their operating companies, Accelsius and AeroFlexx, gearing up for revenue growth inflection in the second half of the year. Innventure's CEO, Bill Haskell, expressed confidence in the companies achieving $1 billion enterprise value opportunities. Accelsius is particularly highlighted for its leading position in the liquid cooling market, engaging with major industry players. The company's financials for March 31, 2025, include total assets of $660,419, cash of $1,375, and liabilities of $105,947. They use non-GAAP financial measures like EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA to assess operational performance. The article also cautions readers with forward-looking statements, mentioning uncertainties and risks associated with future events. For full financial information, the original article on Benzinga.com details the condensed consolidated balance sheets and statements of operations and comprehensive income (loss).
news.headline: Innventure reports Q1 2025 results with Accelsius gaining traction in the liquid cooling market. Revenue growth expected in H2 2025.

news.summary: Super League, a leader in playable media, released its first quarter 2025 financial results, showcasing a strong focus on reducing operating expenses and net losses. The company aims to achieve EBITDA positivity in Q4, with a strategic shift towards streamlined operations and revenue diversification. They acquired Supersocial, a prominent Roblox studio, bolstering their presence in the Roblox ad ecosystem. Super League reported a strong pipeline of almost $20 million in active opportunities across 100 programs. Their consolidated balance sheet as of March 31, 2025, shows total assets at $9.784 million and total liabilities at $13.245 million, with stockholders' equity at ($3.461 million). For more information, visit superleague.com. Total assets: $9,784,000Total liabilities: $13,245,000Stockholders' equity: ($3,461,000)
news.headline: Super League significantly reduces operating expenses and net loss in Q1 2025, on track to achieve EBITDA positive by Q4. Strong pipeline of $20 million in opportunities. Cash at $747,000, assets at $9,784,000, liabilities at $13,245,000, and stockholders' equity at -$3,461,000. Company acquires Supersocial. Webinar scheduled to discuss financial results.

news.summary: Globant, a digital company, announced its first quarter 2025 results with revenues reaching $611.1 million, a 7.0% year-over-year increase. The IFRS Diluted EPS was $0.68, and the Non-IFRS Adjusted Diluted EPS was $1.50. The company had 31,102 employees at the end of the quarter, with revenues coming mainly from North America (55.5%), Latin America (19.6%), Europe (18.2%), and New Markets (6.7%). They served 1,004 customers in the past year, with a significant focus on AI-related opportunities. The company expects revenues of at least $612.0 million for the second quarter and $2,464.0 million for the full year of 2025. The shareholder letter and conference call will provide further details. Non-IFRS financial measures are provided alongside IFRS results, and a caution about forward-looking statements is given due to potential uncertainties.
news.headline: Globant's Q1 2025 revenues hit $611.1 million, up 7.0% YoY, with Gross Profit Margin at 34.9%. CFO expects Q2 to see revenues of at least $612 million. Full-year 2025 estimates revenues of $2,464 million with Non-IFRS Adjusted Diluted EPS to be at least $6.10.(Word count: 100)

news.summary: KORE Group Holdings, Inc. reported positive financial and operational results for the quarter ending March 31, 2025. Total Connections increased to 19.8 million, up 8% from the previous year. Cash provided by operations rose to $2.9 million, a 51% increase, and Free Cash Flow increased to $0.6 million, a 121% rise. Operating Expense decreased to $41.6 million, down 15% due to restructuring efforts. IoT Solutions Margin and IoT Solutions Non-GAAP Margin also showed increases. Revenue breakdown for the quarter showed IoT Connectivity at $53,917 (75%) and IoT Solutions at $18,225 (25%). KORE expects revenue for the year ending December 31, 2025, to be in the range of $288 million to $298 million, with Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $62 million to $67 million, and Free Cash Flow in the range of $10 million to $14 million. A conference call to discuss results and outlook will be held. KORE is a provider of IoT solutions and services. EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA are used as supplemental measures to evaluate operational performance.
news.headline: KORE reports growth in Q1 2025 with 19.8M total connections (up 8%), $2.9M cash from operations (up 51%), $0.6M free cash flow (up 121%), $41.6M operating expense (down 15%), $72.1M total revenue (down 5%), and revenue outlook for 2025 at $288M-$298M. EBITDA expected $62M-$67M, Free Cash Flow at $10M-$14M. Conference call details provided. KORE focuses on IoT solutions and services.

news.summary: China Natural Resources Inc. reported its 2024 financial results, with a net loss decreasing by CNY9.28 million (US$1.27 million) compared to the previous year. Administrative expenses decreased by CNY5.68 million, other income decreased by CNY3.74 million, and fair value gain on financial instruments increased by CNY3.15 million. The loss from discontinued operations also decreased by CNY4.11 million. The Chairman highlighted challenges from trade frictions and geopolitical tensions, but expressed commitment to long-term business strategy. These results reflect the company's focus on exploring further value from the Wulatehouqi Moruogu Tong Mine while navigating economic uncertainties.
news.headline: China Natural Resources Inc. announced 2024 financial results: net loss decreased to US$0.43 million from US$1.27 million in 2023.

news.summary: AleAnna, Inc. reported a basic and diluted net loss per common share of ($0.05) for the first quarter of 2025, showing an improvement from the same period in 2024 where it was ($3.41). The company ended the quarter with cash and cash equivalents of about $27.8 million. Although revenue from Longanesi field production was not recognized in this quarter, the company anticipates reporting it in the upcoming quarter. AleAnna's net loss for Q1 2025 was $2.0 million. The CEO, Marco Brun, expressed positivity about the company's progress and highlighted the importance of the Longanesi field. AleAnna is focused on providing sustainable energy solutions and has several projects in the pipeline, aiming at a potential investment of up to €1.1 billion in the next few years. The company's operations span across Italy and the US. Forward-looking statements and financial risks were also addressed in the press release.
news.headline: AleAnna, Inc. reported a net loss of $2.0 million in Q1 2025 with $27.8 million in cash. Expect revenue from Longanesi field in Q2.

news.summary: Interface, Inc., a leading global flooring solutions company (Nasdaq: TILE), has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.01 per share. This dividend is scheduled to be paid on June 13, 2025, to shareholders of record as of May 30, 2025. Interface, Inc. (Nasdaq: TILE) is a prominent player in sustainable practices and offers a range of carpet tile and resilient flooring products.
news.headline: Interface, Inc. (TILE) declares regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.01 per share, payable on June 13 to shareholders as of May 30.

news.summary: In Greenwood Village, Colorado, it has been disclosed that National Storage Affiliates Trust has declared its quarterly dividends. No specific numbers or details given.
news.headline: National Storage Affiliates Trust in Colorado announces quarterly dividends via Business Wire.

news.summary: Primaris REIT has disclosed that a distribution for May 2025 will be payable on June 16, 2025, to unitholders of record on May 30, 2025. This announcement comes from Primaris Real Estate Investment Trust, trading under the symbol TSX: PMZ.UN. The corrected release replaces the earlier date of May 31, 2025, with the accurate date of May 30, 2025. This adjustment provides clarity on the distribution timeline for investors and stakeholders.
news.headline: Primaris REIT corrects distribution date: payable June 16, 2025 to unitholders of record on May 30, 2025; announced on May 7, 2025.

news.summary: Spokane, Washington - PotlatchDeltic Corporation (Nasdaq: PCH) shared its 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report, showcasing the company's commitment to sustainability and ethical business practices. Eric Cremers, the President, and CEO emphasized the vital role of corporate responsibility in PotlatchDeltic’s operations. The report outlines the achievements and impacts in 2024, reflecting the company's dedication to these values. Cremers stated that the report illustrates some of the notable accomplishments made throughout the year.
news.headline: PotlatchDeltic Corporation releases 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report, showcasing accomplishments and impacts. CEO emphasizes importance.

news.summary: Atlanta-based real estate company GID has revealed a new lease agreement for 150,000 square feet of space with HR solutions provider TriNet. The lease will bring TriNet to High Street, a mixed-use town center district spanning 36 acres in Atlanta's Central Perimeter market. After conducting a search in multiple states, TriNet chose the High Street location. The relocation is anticipated to generate 750 job opportunities within the next five years. This move stands as the largest relocation to Atlanta in recent times.
news.headline: GID leases 150,000 sq ft to TriNet at High Street, bringing 750 jobs to Atlanta in next 5 years.

news.summary: EPR Properties (NYSE:EPR) announced its monthly cash dividend for common shareholders at $0.295 per share. The dividend has a record date of May 30 and will be payable on June 16.
news.headline: EPR Properties declares $0.295 per share monthly dividend for common shareholders, record date May 30, payable June 16.

news.summary: In Boston, American Tower Corporation (NYSE: AMT) declared a quarterly cash distribution of $1.70 per share on its common stock. The distribution will be paid on July 11, 2025, to stockholders of record by June 13, 2025. American Tower, a prominent global REIT, specializes in multitenant communications real estate. With a significant presence in the industry, the company is known for its role as an independent owner, operator, and developer.
news.headline: American Tower Corp declares quarterly distribution of $1.70 per share, payable on July 11, 2025 to stockholders of record on June 13, 2025.

news.summary: San Mateo, California - Essex Property Trust, Inc. disclosed today that its Board of Directors has approved a standard quarterly cash dividend of $2.57 per common share. Shareholders of record as of June 30, 2025, will receive this dividend on July 15, 2025. As an S&P 500 company, Essex Property Trust, Inc. operates as a real estate investment trust (REIT), involved in acquiring, developing, redeveloping, and managing multifamily residential properties in specific locations.
news.headline: Essex Property Trust (NYSE: ESS) declares $2.57/quarter dividend, payable July 15 to shareholders on record as of June 30.

news.summary: Eagle Materials Inc. (NYSE: EXP) has recently named David Rush to its Board of Directors. Rush, previously the Chief Executive Officer of Builders FirstSource (NYSE: BLDR), which is known as the largest supplier in the nation for structural building products, value-added components, and services for the professional market in single-family and multi-family construction, as well as repair and remodeling work. Before leading as CEO, Rush had served in several high-ranking executive positions.
news.headline: Eagle Materials names David Rush to its Board of Directors; Mr. Rush, former CEO of Builders FirstSource, joins.

news.summary: Mativ Holdings, Inc. (MATV) based in Alpharetta, Georgia, revealed its involvement in two significant investor conferences. The first one, Stifel 2025 Boston Cross Sector 1x1 Conference, will be held on June 3, 2025, in Boston, Massachusetts. Following that, the company will take part in the Deutsche Bank 2025 Global Industrials, Materials & Building Products Conference on June 4, 2025, in New York, New York. Mativ expressed gratitude for the interest shown and eagerly anticipates active participation in these events. Mativ Holdings, Inc. positions itself as a prominent global entity focused on specializations within the specified sectors.
news.headline: Mativ Holdings (NYSE: MATV) to attend investor conferences: Stifel 2025 Boston Conference on June 3, 2025 and Deutsche Bank Industrials Conference on June 4, 2025.

news.summary: TILT Holdings Inc., a cannabis business solutions provider, reported financial results for Q1 2025. Revenue was $22.7 million, down from $37.5 million in the prior year. Gross profit was $3.4 million with a gross margin of 14.9%. Net loss was $13.2 million compared to $9.7 million in the prior year. Adjusted EBITDA was $(974) thousand versus $38 thousand before. Cash flow from operations was $1.9 million. The company had $4.3 million in cash and equivalents as of March 31, 2025, flat from the end of 2024. The company announced the sale of two Massachusetts retail stores for $2 million and obtained EU certification for its vaporizer. They shifted production to Indonesia and hosted a conference call to discuss results. TILT's core business, Jupiter Research, focuses on vaporization products and has operations in several countries. This quarter marked a shift towards an asset-light operating model to drive growth and profitability.
news.headline: TILT Holdings Inc. reports Q1 2025 results: Revenue $22.7M, Gross profit $3.4M, Net loss $13.2M, Adjusted EBITDA $(974)K. Jupitor focuses on innovation & steady demand. Company aims for sustained growth.

news.summary: SRx Health Solutions Inc. reported its first-quarter results for 2025, with significant improvements in key financial metrics. Their net revenue was $7.2 million, indicating a 9% decrease year-over-year due to the closure of a business channel. The company managed to reduce its net loss by 66% to $(1.0) million, with an adjusted EBITDA 1 loss of $(0.6) million, a 54% year-over-year improvement. The adjusted EBITDA margin 1 also improved by 865 basis points to (9)%. The company's gross margin remained at 33%, and their liquidity stood at $1.1 million in cash and equivalents as of March 31, 2025. SRx Health Solutions is positive about future growth and has undergone structural and operational changes to move towards profitability. The Company recently merged with SRx Health and changed its name. They also outlined their digital-first strategy and plans for optimized growth in the future. Management will discuss these results further in a conference call and webcast. SRx Health Solutions, an integrated Canadian healthcare services provider, continues to focus on enhancing patient care initiatives and wellness programs.
news.headline: SRx Health Solutions improved financials in Q1 2025: EPS up 90% to $(0.37), Adjusted EBITDA loss improved 54% to $(0.6)M. Total net sales reached $7.2M, down 9% due to channel closing in 2024, but net loss decreased by 66% to $(1.0)M. Company's liquidity reported cash and cash equivalents of $1.1M as of March 31, 2025. SRx optimistic on future with focus on profitable growth. Stock symbol: SRXH. Website for detailed info.

news.summary: Expion360 Inc., a leader in lithium-ion battery power storage, reported their financial results for Q1 2025 with revenue totaling $2.0 million, up 111% from Q1 2024 and 5th consecutive quarter of growth. They started shipping their e360 Home Energy Storage Solutions and closed a $2.6 million offering. The CEO highlighted the robust revenue growth, new OEM partnerships, and distributor relationships. The net loss improved by 48% due to higher sales and decreased expenses. Cash equivalents were $1.1 million as of March 31, 2025. They made efforts to mitigate tariffs and diversify the supply chain. The company is looking to expand sales, develop new technologies, and onshore manufacturing to the US. Expion360 offers premium lithium iron phosphate batteries for various applications and is headquartered in Redmond, Oregon. The company's products are available through more than 300 dealers, wholesalers, private-label customers, and OEMs. Expion360 aims to continue growth initiatives and introduce new technologies and products.
news.headline: Expion360's Q1 2025 revenue grew by 111% to $2.0 million, marking 5th consecutive quarter of growth. Company began shipping e360 Home Energy Storage Solutions and completed a $2.6 million offering. Profit increased to $0.5 million and net loss decreased by 48% to $1.2 million. Cash and equivalents rose to $1.1 million. Expion360 is focused on expanding sales and introducing new technologies.

news.summary: Fold Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: FLD) reported a strong first quarter for 2025, with revenue at $7.1 million - a 44% increase year-over-year. Their Bitcoin Treasury Holdings rose to 1,490 BTC, a 50% QoQ increase. They also launched a Bitcoin Gift Card that provides access to thousands of retailers and saw a 300% increase in new accounts and a 67% rise in platform volumes. Fold aims to expand its Custody and Trading business, increase their bitcoin holdings, and introduce new initiatives like the Fold Bitcoin Rewards Credit Card. With a 2025 full-year revenue projection of $61.6 million, Fold plans to increase marketing expenses by 10x from 2024. Their recent announcements and strategic plans signal progress and growth prospects for the company. Mr. Will Reeves, Chairman and CEO of Fold, expressed optimism and commitment to Bitcoin, positioning the company as a key player in the Bitcoin financial services sector.
news.headline: Fold Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: FLD) Q1 2025 results: Revenue $7.1M, 44% YoY increase, Bitcoin Treasury Holdings: 1,490 BTC, 50% increase from Q4 2024. New accounts up over 300%, total accounts added 17,000 new accounts, total verified accounts added 5,000 in the quarter. CEO optimistic about growth prospects with new product initiatives like Fold Bitcoin Gift Card. Bitcoin treasury value over $150M. Strategic updates include launching Fold Credit Card with 75,000 waitlist, expecting nationwide launch this year. Strategic outlook: Revenue expectation $61.6M in 2025, marketing expenses $3M. Full story on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Legacy Education Inc., a provider of career-focused education, reported financial results for the third fiscal quarter ending March 31, 2025, with revenue up 50.7% to $18.6 million. New student starts increased by 70.7%, reaching a student population of 3,245. EBITDA was $3.8 million, adjusted EBITDA at $3.9 million, net income of $2.8 million, and diluted earnings per share at $0.21. For the nine months ending March 31, 2025, revenue grew 39% to $46.2 million, with new student starts up by 30.6%. EBITDA stood at $8.3 million, adjusted EBITDA at $8.6 million, net income of $6.3 million, and diluted earnings per share at $0.51. Legacy Education's CEO, LeeAnn Rohmann, attributed the success to strategic focus and increased demand for career-focused education. Expenses also rose to support enrolment growth, with educational services spending up 39.3% to $24.8 million and general/administrative expenses rising by 32.7% to $12.9 million for the nine months ending March 31, 2025. Marketing expenses notably increased alongside revenue growth.
news.headline: Legacy Education Inc. achieved record Q3 with 50% revenue growth, $18.6 million revenue, and 3,245 enrolled students.

news.summary: BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics Inc. announced financial results for Q1 2025 and a corporate update, with a focus on advancing its NurOwn development program for ALS treatment. The company submitted an IND amendment to the FDA for the Phase 3b trial, which aims to enroll approximately 200 ALS patients. In Q1 2025, cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash were around $1.8 million. R&D expenses were $1.3 million, while general/administrative expenses were $1.8 million. Net loss for Q1 2025 was approximately $2.9 million. A conference call and webcast were rescheduled to May 19, 2025, at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time. Key participant numbers were provided for the event. Overall, BrainStorm is focusing on the development of its NurOwn technology platform for neurodegenerative diseases, particularly ALS. Some recent highlights include presenting data at the ISCT 2025 Meeting and the 2025 ALS Drug Development Summit. Their lead therapy NurOwn has shown promise with valuable insights into ALS disease biology and biomarkers. The company is also working on an exosome-based platform for regenerative medicine. BrainStorm urges caution due to inherent uncertainties and risks in their forward-looking statements.
news.headline: BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics reported Q1 2025 financial results: $1.8M cash, $1.3M R&D expenses, $1.8M admin expenses, $2.9M net loss. Brewing investor call on May 19.

news.summary: LightPath Technologies, Inc. announced financial results for its fiscal third quarter ended March 31, 2025. Revenue increased by 19.1% to $9.2 million, with gross profit reaching $2.7 million, a 65.9% increase, and operating expenses at $6.0 million, a 42.9% increase. The net loss was $3.6 million. They closed the acquisition of G5 Infrared, securing orders worth $2.2 million, $4.8 million, and $4.9 million in the defense industry. The company participated in various industry and investor conferences. LightPath aims to become a leading provider of infrared imaging solutions, especially emphasized by President and CEO Sam Rubin. The company also provided non-GAAP financial measures, including adjusted EBITDA with a loss of $2.0 million in the third quarter of fiscal 2025. The company will host an earnings call to discuss further details and future plans.
news.headline: LightPath Technologies reported financial results for Q3 FY2025 with revenue of $9.2M, gross profit of $2.7M, and a net loss of $3.6M, showcasing growth in revenue and margin. Key highlights include the acquisition of G5 Infrared, along with significant orders from L3Harris Technologies and defense customers, driving strategic vision in the infrared imaging market. Operating expenses increased to $6.0M, impacting the bottom line. Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA was a loss of $2.0M. Management is optimistic about future growth, particularly in defense programs and product portfolio. Email: recap this: LightPath Technologies Q3 FY2025 financial results highlight revenue growth, strategic acquisitions and defense program outlook. Key numbers: Revenue $9.2M, Gross Profit $2.7M, Net Loss $3.6M, adj. EBITDA loss $2.0M.

news.summary: Quantum Computing Inc. (QCi) released financial results for Q1 2025, reporting revenues of approximately $39,000 with a 33% gross margin, up from $27,000 in Q1 2024. Operating expenses were $8.3 million, higher than the previous year, leading to a net income of $17.0 million, compared to a net loss of $6.4 million in the prior year. Total assets increased to $242.5 million, cash reserves rose to $166.4 million, liabilities decreased to $21.7 million, and shareholders' equity reached $220.8 million by March 31, 2025. QCi completed its Quantum Photonic Chip Foundry in Arizona, received five initial orders, and raised $93.6 million through a private placement offering. The company announced partnerships, Board appointments, and sales to NASA and academic/industrial customers. The Earnings Conference Call is scheduled for May 15, 2025. QCi aims to capitalize on the growing demand for quantum solutions across various sectors.
news.headline: Quantum Computing Inc. (QCi) reports Q1 2025 financial results: Revenues $39,000, net income $17.0 million. Assets $242.5 million.

news.summary: Fortress Biotech, Inc. announced its Q1 2025 financial results and corporate highlights including the FDA accepting its New Drug Application for CUTX-101, aiming for a PDUFA goal date of Sept 30, 2025. A special meeting on May 28, 2025, will approve Fortress' subsidiary Checkpoint Therapeutics' acquisition by Sun Pharma for ~$28 million. Journey Medical launched Emrosi for rosacea treatment in March, with net product revenues of $13.1 million in Q1. CUTX-101 development, clinical trials, and financial data were also detailed, showing a consolidated cash increase to $91.3 million as of March 31, 2025. Fortress' business model focuses on developing innovative therapies for long-term shareholder value.
news.headline: Fortress Biotech reports Q1 2025 financial results: $13.1M net product revenues; $12.7M net loss; $91.3M cash & equivalents. Acquisition and FDA updates also mentioned.

news.summary: Park Aerospace Corp. reported its financial results for the 2025 fiscal year fourth quarter and full fiscal year, ended March 2, 2025. The company's net sales for the fourth quarter were $16,939,000, up from $16,333,000 in the previous year, and $62,026,000 for the full fiscal year, up from $56,004,000 in the previous year. Net earnings for the fourth quarter were $1,246,000, down from $2,670,000 in the previous year, and $5,882,000 for the full year, down from $7,473,000 in the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter was $3,418,000, up from $3,201,000 in the previous year, and $11,649,000 for the full year, up from $10,989,000 in the previous year. The company discussed various charges and special items affecting its financial results. Park Aerospace Corp. conducts conference calls to discuss its financial results and other matters.
news.headline: Park Aerospace Corp. reported net sales of $16.9M for Q4 2025, $62M for full year. Earnings $1.2M for Q4, $5.9M for full year. Adjusted EBITDA $3.4M for Q4, $11.6M for full year. Full story on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Greenlane Renewables Inc. reported their first-quarter financial results for 2025, showcasing revenue of $7.0 million, gross profit of $2.6 million, an adjusted EBITDA loss of $1.1 million, and a net loss of $1.0 million. They maintain a sales order backlog of $21.2 million and cash reserves of $16.2 million as of March 31, 2025. The CEO, Brad Douville, highlighted their progress in executing the 2025 strategic plan focusing on renewable natural gas projects. The company is working on biogas desulfurization and landfill gas upgrading technologies to lower costs and enhance performance. They are also deepening strategic partnerships and looking at localizing manufacturing in the U.S. and Brazil for long-term growth. The CFO, Stephanie Mason, noted their strong cash position and backlog conversion to profitable revenue. The company's three main strategic pillars for 2025 include sales growth in profitable segments, development of next-gen landfill gas upgrading products, and improving core upgrading systems profitability. The global biogas market outlook remains optimistic, with biogas being viewed as essential for addressing climate change and energy challenges. The American Biogas Council has launched a significant advertising campaign to promote biogas.
news.headline: Greenlane Renewables Inc. reports $7.0M revenue and $2.6M gross profit in Q1 of 2025. Sales backlog $21.2M, cash reserves $16.2M with no debt. CEO mentions strategic initiatives to focus on RNG projects and new technologies for growth. Market outlook: biogas plays vital role in climate change.

news.summary: The article discusses the latest updates from Solid Biosciences Inc., a company specializing in genetic medicines for neuromuscular and cardiac diseases. In the first quarter of 2025, the company reported having $306.9 million in cash, with a runway expected into the first half of 2027. Significant developments include ongoing participant dosing in the Phase 1/2 INSPIRE DUCHENNE trial for Duchenne muscular dystrophy, anticipated dosing for SGT-212 for Friedreich's Ataxia in the second half of 2025, and FDA IND submission for SGT-501 for Catecholaminergic Polymorphic Ventricular Tachycardia in the first half of 2025. The company is focused on advancing gene therapy candidates and platform technologies to address various rare diseases. The update includes financial highlights, research and development expenses, and net loss figures. CEO Bo Cumbo emphasizes the importance of safety, promising developments in clinical data, and future aspirations in gene therapy advancements. For detailed information, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Solid Biosciences reported Q1 2025 financial results with $306.9M cash; progressing gene therapy trials for Duchenne, FA, CPVT.

news.summary: Rein Therapeutics has initiated the RENEW Phase 2 trial of LTI-03 in patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) and expects topline interim data by the first half of 2026. The trial aims to enroll approximately 120 patients for two dose cohorts of LTI-03 or placebo. The company has collaborated with Qureight for deep-learning AI image analysis and obtained patents for novel formulation and administration methods. The company reported cash and cash equivalents of $7.4 million as of March 31, 2025, with financial resources expected to last through September 2025. Research and Development expenses were $3.1 million, and General and Administrative expenses were $2.5 million for the first quarter of 2025. Rein Therapeutics is focusing on developing first-in-class therapies for orphan pulmonary and fibrosis indications, with LTI-03 and LTI-01 being the lead product candidates. The company aims to address significant unmet medical needs through its innovative pipeline.
news.headline: Rein Therapeutics announces Q1 2025 financial report, initiation of RENEW Phase 2 trial for LTI-03 in IPF, and cash position of $7.4 million.

news.summary: TuHURA Biosciences, Inc., a Phase 3 immune-oncology company, reported financial results and a corporate update for Q1 2025. They announced the initiation of a Phase 1b/2a trial of their IFx-Hu2.0 drug with Keytruda in MCCUP patients and anticipates starting a Phase 3 trial in advanced MCC in Q2 2025. They also plan to acquire Kineta, Inc. and initiate a Phase 2 trial of Kineta's VISTA inhibiting mAb in NPM1-mutated AML in Q3 2025. The company aims to present data on tumor microenvironment modulators targeting MDSCs and M2 macrophages. Key points include the initiation of clinical trials, acquisition plans, and future research directions to expand treatment options for various cancers.
news.headline: TuHURA Biosciences reported Q1 2025 financial results, plans to start Phase 3 trial of IFx-Hu2.0, and acquire Kineta, Inc. & initiate Phase 2 trial in Q2-Q3 2025.

news.summary: SOS Limited reported a 92.6% surge in commodity trading revenue to $214.3 million in FY 2024, with cryptocurrency mining revenue halving to $9.3 million due to facility upgrades in Texas. Revenue from hosting services and other segments also decreased. The company holds 736.75 units of BTC and 2,924.79 units of ETH, focusing on product lines like sesame, sulfur, rubber, and more. Revenue costs increased by $146.2 million, and operating expenses rose by $9.6 million in 2024. Selling expenses increased to $2.8 million, general and administrative expenses rose by $7.1 million, mainly due to mining rig depreciation. SOS is transitioning towards commodity trading, with its services breakdown showing a focus on this area at 92.6%. Operating expenses increased by 50.6% year-on-year, while revenue sources included commodity trading, cryptocurrency mining, hosting services, and other segments, with commodity trading representing 92.6% of total revenue. SOS is resuming full production after facility upgrades.
news.headline: SOS Limited's FY 2024 sees 92.6% surge in commodity trading revenue to $214.3M; cryptocurrency mining halves to $9.3M. Expenses increase significantly, with revenue costs growing to $224.4M and operating expenses rising to $28.6M. Operating income affected by upgrades and expansions, yet SOS remains firm on its strategic pivot towards commodity trading. SOS holds 736.75 units of BTC and 2,924.79 units of ETH as of Dec 31, 2024.

news.summary: Safe Pro Group Inc. (Nasdaq: SPAI) based in AVENTURA, Fla., which offers artificial intelligence (AI)-driven security solutions, has released its corporate update for the first quarter of 2025. The period signifies a significant shift for Safe Pro as it has started to establish strong connections with U.S. government officials. The Safe Pro Object Threat Detection platform has garnered substantial attention, leading to the preparation of various joint bids. This uptick in interest has set the stage for potential growth opportunities for the company.
news.headline: Safe Pro Group reports Q1 2025 results, gaining traction with U.S. govt. stakeholders; interest in Safe Pro Object Threat Detection increases.
